symptoms.symptom_name: Widespread thick, well-demarcated erythematous plaques with dense silvery-white scale over extensor elbows/knees, scalp, lower back and trunk

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Well-demarcated erythematous plaques with adherent silvery scale in an extensor/scalp/truncal distribution is the classic hallmark of plaque psoriasis.
Seborrhoeic dermatitis produces greasy yellowish scale in a central-facial/scalp distribution, not thick adherent silvery scale on extensor surfaces.

symptoms.symptom_name: Rigid, tight plaques fissuring into deep painful bleeding cracks on movement, causing functional immobility

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confluent, thickened plaques become rigid and fissure under mechanical tension across flexures — a marker of flare severity and the source of the mechanical pain and immobility.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known 10-year history of the same condition, previously limited to elbows and knees

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An established chronic diagnosis with a typical extensor distribution strongly anchors the current flare as the same disease rather than a new eruption.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent streptococcal throat infection and severe psychosocial stress preceding the flare

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Streptococcal infection (superantigen-driven) and psychological stress are classic, well-recognised triggers of a psoriasis flare.

symptoms.symptom_name: New plaque at the site of a recent forearm scratch (Koebner / isomorphic phenomenon)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The Koebner phenomenon — new psoriatic plaques at sites of skin trauma — is characteristic of active psoriasis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Systemically well, normothermic, no rigors; redness in discrete plaques with normal intervening skin (not confluent whole-body erythema)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Erythroderma requires confluent erythema over >90% BSA with systemic instability; discrete plaques with normal intervening skin and a well patient argue strongly against it (though precise BSA cannot be measured on video).
Absence of fever and rigors makes established skin sepsis unlikely at presentation.

symptoms.symptom_name: No pustules, and no purulent weeping/oozing or crusting from the cracks

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The defining feature of pustular psoriasis is crops of sterile pustules; their absence argues strongly against it.
Purulent weeping/crusting would signal secondary infection; its absence makes established infection unlikely at presentation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Auspitz sign — pinpoint capillary bleeding on lifting a scale

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pinpoint bleeding from dilated dermal papillary capillaries when a scale is removed is a classic confirmatory sign of psoriasis — but it requires the clinician's hands and is unavailable on telehealth.
Nail pitting is a common associated feature of psoriasis that supports the diagnosis; assessment requires close in-person inspection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Skin swab / streptococcal serology / inflammatory bloods to confirm trigger or exclude secondary infection

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A wound swab and bloods would confirm or exclude secondary bacterial infection; not obtainable on telehealth, so the telehealth assessment remains presumptive and safety-net-dependent.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Widespread silvery-scaled extensor plaques + rigid fissuring + a known chronic history + a fresh Koebner plaque together make the benign (non-emergency) severe flare confident enough for presumptive telehealth management.

Discrete (not confluent) plaques + no pustules + no purulent weeping + systemically well together down-weight the three emergency variants and justify a T2 disposition with a mandatory safety-net rather than emergency transfer.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person dermatological examination (Auspitz sign, BSA estimate, nail and pustule inspection)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: A hands-on examination confirms the diagnosis, quantifies severity/BSA and excludes the emergency variants — appropriately arranged as an urgent-routine dermatology/GP review, not obtainable via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Wound swab / inflammatory bloods if secondary infection suspected
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Indicated only if fever, purulent weeping or systemic features develop; confirms or excludes secondary bacterial infection.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent ED work-up / blood cultures now
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The patient is systemically well with a stable flare; ordering an emergency septic work-up now would be over-investigation. It becomes appropriate only if the safety-net escalation triggers fire.
